text: The only way to encourage innovation is to give it to the young. The young have a great advantage in that they are ignorant… I think ignorance in science is very important. If you’re like me and you know too much, you can’t try new things. I always work in fields of which I’m totally ignorant.
author: Sydney Brenner

text: There are two kinds of scientific progress: the methodical experimentation and categorization which gradually extend the boundaries of knowledge, and the revolutionary leap of genius which redefines and transcends those boundaries.
Acknowledging our debt to the former, we yearn nonetheless for the latter.
author: Prokhor Zakharov

text: Imagination is the Discovering Faculty, pre-eminently. It is that which penetrates into the unseen worlds around us, the worlds of Science.
author: Ada Lovelace

text: Nothing is too wonderful to be true, if it be consistent with the laws of nature.
author: Michael Faraday

text: Innovation comes only from an assault on the unknown.
author: Sydney Brenner

text: Creativity in science, as in the arts, cannot be organised. It arises spontaneously from individual talent. Well-run laboratories can foster it, but hierarchical organisation, inflexible, bureaucratic rules, and mountains of futile paperwork can kill it.
Discoveries cannot be planned; they pop up, like Puck, in unexpected places.
author: Max Perutz

text: Science is the belief in the ignorance of experts.
author: Richard Feynman

text: The scientist does not study nature because it is useful; he studies it because he delights in it, and he delights in it because it is beautiful.
author: Henri Poincaré

text: The important thing in science is not so much to obtain new facts as to discover new ways of thinking about them.
author: William Lawrence Bragg

text: Scientific progress on a broad front results from the free play of free intellects, working on subjects of their own choice, in the manner dictated by their curiosity for exploration of the unknown.
author: Vannevar Bush

text: Imagination is more important than knowledge.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Nothing in life is to be feared, it is only to be understood. Now is the time to understand more, so that we may fear less.
author: Marie Curie

text: You can kill or mutilate the advance of science, you cannot shape it.
author: Michael Polanyi
